content: When adding admin authorization to existing Laravel apps, common pitfalls include: (1) Forgetting to register Gate::define() in a service provider — the gate exists in code but never gets loaded. (2) Using middleware('can:') on route groups without first ensuring 'auth' middleware runs — unauthenticated users hit the gate and get unexpected errors instead of redirects. (3) FormRequest::authorize() returning true when it should check specific permissions — the current TenantStoreRequest returns (bool)$this->user() which authorizes ANY authenticated user. (4) Not protecting artisan commands that create super-admin users from unauthorized execution. (5) Gate::before() callback that grants all permissions to admin but forgetting to scope it to specific checks — accidentally giving admin access to tenant-scoped operations too.
